audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for opening, no lack in grace-gift, divisions, baptism/factions, cross as foolishness/power, wisdom overturned, boasting in Lord.

verses.restored: Paul, called as apostle of Messiah Jesus through the will of the Divine, and Sosthenes the sibling,

to the assembly of the Divine that is in Corinth, to those sanctified in Messiah Jesus, called holy ones, with all those in every place calling upon the name of our Lord Jesus Messiah, both theirs and ours:
Grace to you and peace from Divine our Father and Lord Jesus Messiah.
I thank my Divine always concerning you for the grace of the Divine given to you in Messiah Jesus,
because in everything you were enriched in him, in every word and all knowledge,
just as the witness of Messiah was confirmed among you,
so that you are not lacking in any grace-gift as you await the unveiling of our Lord Jesus Messiah,
who will also confirm you until the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Messiah.
Trustworthy is the Divine, through whom you were called into fellowship/participation of his Son, Jesus Messiah our Lord.

I urge you, siblings, through the name of our Lord Jesus Messiah, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no splits among you, but that you be restored together in the same mind and the same discernment.
For it was made clear to me concerning you, my siblings, by those of Chloe, that there are strifes among you.
I mean this: each of you says, I am of Paul; I of Apollos; I of Cephas; I of Messiah.
Has Messiah been divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?
I thank the Divine that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
so that no one may say you were baptized into my name.
I also baptized the household of Stephanas; beyond that I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.
For Messiah did not send me to baptize, but to announce good news, not in wisdom of word, so that the cross of Messiah might not be emptied.
For the word of the cross is foolishness to those perishing, but to us being saved/healed it is power of the Divine.
For it is written: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the understanding of the understanding ones I will set aside.
Where is the wise one? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Did not the Divine make foolish the wisdom of the world?
For since in the wisdom of the Divine the world through wisdom did not know the Divine, the Divine was pleased through the foolishness of proclamation to save/heal those trusting.
Since Judeans ask for signs and Greeks seek wisdom,
but we proclaim Messiah crucified, to Judeans a stumbling block and to nations foolishness,
but to those called, both Judeans and Greeks, Messiah power of the Divine and wisdom of the Divine.
Because the foolishness of the Divine is wiser than humans, and the weakness of the Divine is stronger than humans.
For see your calling, siblings: not many wise according to flesh, not many powerful, not many well-born.
But the foolish things of the world the Divine chose to shame the wise; and the weak things of the world the Divine chose to shame the strong.
The low-born things of the world and the despised the Divine chose, the things that are not, to make inactive the things that are,
so that no flesh might boast before the Divine.
From him you are in Messiah Jesus, who became wisdom to us from the Divine, and righteousness/justice and sanctification and redemption/release,
so that, just as it is written: Let the one boasting boast in the Lord.

translation_notes: Schisma is rendered split/division to preserve body-fracture language.
The cross is framed as power and wisdom that overturn status competition, not as a slogan for anti-intellectualism.
